title: Agent Verdict
description: Canonical AgentVerdict shape returned by the framework's verdict-emitting agents — the 13 Phase 5 retrofit reviewers/analysts and every reviewer/analyst that follows them. Resolves TR-0005 by pinning {agent, status, confidence, findings}: `status` is preserved from the original {agent, status, ...} family so the stamp gate is unaffected, `confidence` is the planning-session AgentVerdict's required addition, and `findings` generalises the older `errors`/`evidence` arrays. NOTE: the two Phase 1 scope-verifiers (validation-objective-verifier, validation-completion-verifier) emit a richer {agent, status, errors, classifications} variant that feeds /validate's stamp flow directly; that variant predates this contract and is deliberately out of this schema's scope (see TR-0005).

agent.description: The emitting agent's name — must match the agent-frontmatter name pattern (PSF scope prefix). Ties a verdict back to a registered agent node.

status.description: Overall verdict. `pass` = no blocking finding (or, for analysts, a confident conclusion); `fail` = at least one blocking finding, or an inconclusive analysis. The stamp gate keys on this field.

confidence.description: The agent's confidence in its verdict, in [0,1]. Below-threshold verdicts (convention: < 0.7) escalate to human review rather than asserting the result.

findings.description: Zero or more findings. An empty array with `status: pass` is the clean verdict. Each finding localises an issue and, for reviewers, suggests a fix the human applies.

path.description: Where the finding lives — `file:line`, or a logical locus (a domain name, `commit-msg`, `.gitignore`) for agents that do not review file content.

severity.description: Finding severity. A `critical` or `major` finding drives `status: fail`; `minor` findings are advisory.

fix.description: Suggested remediation. For verdict-only reviewers this is advisory (the human applies it); for auto-fixers it records what was applied.

fixed.description: True when an auto-fixer (worktree-isolated) already applied the fix; the verdict then records what the worktree contains.

rule.description: Originating rule identifier, when the finding came from a tool (a linter rule id, etc.).
